So a few more key distinctions guys It's far easier to retain a players than it is to replace them same way as it It's harder to generate new customers than it is to resell old customers Well, it's harder to recruit than it is to just retain And then the biggest mistake here is that don't think just because they're making a lot of money that they're gonna keep selling for you Okay, I Was making a lot of money for one selling position Several years back and left to make more money With a different selling position and it's not always about the money The last selling position I was at I was making 30 to 40 grand a month free and clear And I left that right and that's a lot of money that coming in as net income So and I didn't leave because I wanted to go make more money doing something else That's not it at all So just don't think that it's all about the money the money and compensation plans are important But that's not everything and also don't think that because they're selling well that they're at 30 to 40 percent or 50 percent Or whatever they didn't need attention and then you can just leave them be and okay I'm not gonna bother that person just because they're doing good. Obviously, you're not gonna micromanage that person But at the stead instead Make sure that you're still investing into your best guys Right make sure you're still leading them giving them attention and leading them both so they can achieve their goals within the company And they have a clear vision and also outside of the company as well and they can grow as a person So moving on here I wrote down some key questions and this is how short this training is but some just some key cup questions to get you guys thinking about this and Give you some quick tips on leadership and then we can move out of this training But the first one is you know, where are they in terms of clarity necessity and inspiration?
